spec_match.rationale: Reads `screenPixelRatio` and turns it into `downscaleFactor`/`frameRate` track constraints sized to a bandwidth budget — the practical authoring shape the spec was added to enable.

interactive.rationale: Range slider + budget select + start-capture button with 4 listeners drive a live calculation of recommended constraints, then run a real capture and show actual settings after.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises `the calculator picks downscaleFactor and frame-rate constraints to match. Start a capture and compare ...`. Page covers calculation + start + compare.

edge_cases.rationale: Calculator handles preset budgets but no probe for `screenPixelRatio` support — pre-feature browsers fall through silently. Also no demo of mismatch (recommended constraints exceed what the surface can deliver).

references.rationale: `see also` now cites `https://chromestatus.com/feature/6738236535472128` and the Screen Capture `screenPixelRatio` spec section.

design_system.rationale: 30 CSS variable references, no raw hex, no button override mismatch.

openQuestions.title: Add a feature-detect probe
openQuestions.detail: No probe means a pre-feature browser will silently produce wrong recommendations. Add `typeof MediaTrackSettings.prototype.screenPixelRatio !== 'undefined'`-style probe with a banner.

openQuestions.title: Surface constraint mismatch
openQuestions.detail: When the recommended constraints exceed what the captured surface can deliver, `applyConstraints` may resolve but produce unexpected `getSettings()`. Compare recommended vs actual side by side and call out drift.

openQuestions.title: Add a custom-budget input
openQuestions.detail: The preset budgets cover the common cases but a power user wants to specify a Kbps target directly. Add a numeric input that drives the calculator alongside the presets.

summary: Useful planner-tool shape that combines the new screenPixelRatio reading with practical bandwidth budgeting, with the required ChromeStatus/spec references now present. It still needs a feature-detect probe and constraint-mismatch readout.
